task_completion.description: Create the conditions CSV file with the required columns and condition combinations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet or text editor showing a file containing the columns 'cue_location', 'target_location', 'cue_validity', and 'corrAns', with rows covering valid, invalid, and neutral conditions for both left and right targets.

task_completion.description: Set up the experiment flow in PsychoPy Builder with instructions, trial components, a loop, and a debrief routine.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PsychoPy Builder Flow panel showing routines for instructions, trial sequence (fixation, cue, target), a loop encompassing the trial routine(s), and a debrief routine.

task_completion.description: Configure the correct durations for the fixation, cue, and blank ISI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PsychoPy component properties dialogs showing a fixation duration of 0.5s (500ms), a cue duration of 0.2s (200ms), and a 0.1s (100ms) blank ISI (either as a separate component or as a delay before the target starts).

task_completion.description: Configure the target timeout and keyboard response collection with specific keys.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PsychoPy component properties showing the target stimulus with a 2.0s duration/timeout, and a Keyboard response component with allowed keys explicitly set to 'left' and 'right'.

task_completion.description: Save the PsychoPy experiment to the specified file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PsychoPy window title bar or a 'Save As' dialog showing the experiment saved as 'posner_cueing_experiment.psyexp' in the '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to construct the experiment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the PsychoPy application open and being actively interacted with (adding routines, editing components), rather than just writing a .psyexp XML file in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent created the conditions CSV file using an editor or spreadsheet application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a text editor (e.g., nano, gedit) or spreadsheet tool (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) open with the conditions data being typed or formatted.

integrity.description: Verify the experiment was saved genuinely through the PsychoPy interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog box in PsychoPy being used to specify the filename and directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target PsychoPy experiment file path is /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/posner_cueing_experiment.psyexp and the expected conditions CSV file path is /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/conditions/posner_conditions.csv. The task requires the agent to build the experiment from scratch without provided data files.
